unit: Ὅ6 Unit 6: Systems of Linear Equations and Inequalities

questions.question: A shipping company has two types of trucks. The first type can carry $3x$ packages and travel $2y$ miles. The second type can carry $2x$ packages and travel $3y$ miles. If the company has $240$ packages to deliver and $420$ miles of road to cover, what is the value of $x$?

A traffic management system uses $x$ cameras and $y$ sensors to monitor traffic flow. Each camera can monitor $5x$ cars and $2y$ roads, while each sensor can monitor $3x$ cars and $4y$ roads. If the system can monitor $150$ cars and $80$ roads, what is the value of $y$?

A delivery company uses two types of vehicles: cars and trucks. Cars travel at $x$ miles per hour and trucks travel at $y$ miles per hour. If a car travels $120$ miles and a truck travels $180$ miles in the same time, what is the ratio of $x$ to $y$?

A logistics company has $x$ warehouses and $y$ delivery trucks. Each warehouse can store $2x$ packages and each truck can carry $3y$ packages. If the company has $480$ packages to store and $720$ packages to deliver, what is the value of $y$?

A transportation system uses $x$ buses and $y$ trains to transport people. Each bus can carry $4x$ people and each train can carry $5y$ people. If the system can transport $240$ people and the ratio of buses to trains is $3:2$, what is the value of $x$?

A shipping company has two types of containers: $20$-foot and $40$-foot. The company has $x$ $20$-foot containers and $y$ $40$-foot containers. If the company has $120$ feet of space and $40$ containers to ship, what is the value of $x$?

A traffic flow system uses $x$ traffic lights and $y$ pedestrian crossings. Each traffic light can control $3x$ cars and $2y$ pedestrians, while each pedestrian crossing can control $2x$ cars and $3y$ pedestrians. If the system can control $180$ cars and $120$ pedestrians, what is the value of $y$?

A delivery company uses $x$ vans and $y$ motorcycles to deliver packages. Each van can carry $5x$ packages and travel $2y$ miles, while each motorcycle can carry $2x$ packages and travel $3y$ miles. If the company has $300$ packages to deliver and $420$ miles of road to cover, what is the value of $y$?

A logistics company has $x$ warehouses and $y$ delivery trucks. Each warehouse can store $2x$ packages and each truck can carry $3y$ packages. If the company has $600$ packages to store and $900$ packages to deliver, find the values of $x$ and $y$.

A transportation system uses $x$ buses and $y$ trains to transport people. Each bus can carry $4x$ people and each train can carry $5y$ people. If the system can transport $360$ people and the ratio of buses to trains is $2:3$, find the values of $x$ and $y$ and interpret the results in context.

teacher_rules: Use a graphing calculator to check your work.
Consider the units of the variables in each problem.
Think about the real-world implications of your solutions.
